name: Ermita de Nuestra Senora de Gracia - circular from Fuente Obejuna
name_original: Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Gracia – circular desde Fuente Obejuna
region_intro: Jogging routes around Fuente Obejuna traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the dehesa, an agroforestry system of holm oaks, cork oaks, and grasslands. The region features the Guadiato River, offering scenic riverside paths, and the Sierra Boyera Reservoir. Terrain varies from open fields and shaded woodlands to compacted earth trails, providing a range of surfaces for running. Best jogging routes around Fuente Obejuna The most popular jogging route is Vía Verde La Maquinilla – circular between Belmez and Peñarroya, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route follows a former railway line through dehesa landscapes. Another top favourite among local runners is Ermita de Nuestra Senora de Gracia - circular from Fuente Obejuna, an easy 2.5 miles (4.0 km) path. This circular route offers a quick run from Fuente Obejuna, suitable for a shorter outing. Local runners also love the Running loop from Granja de Torrehermosa, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through varied local terrain, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Fuente Obejuna is defined by dehesa landscapes, the Guadiato River, and the Vía Verde, offering a mix of natural beauty and historical interest. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Fuente Obejuna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Fuente Obejuna?
A: The running trails in Fuente Obejuna offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find paths traversing the unique &lt;strong&gt;dehesa landscape&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by open woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks interspersed with grasslands. Many routes, like sections of the Vía Verde, feature &lt;strong&gt;compacted earth&lt;/strong&gt;, ideal for running. You can also expect scenic riverside paths along the Guadiato River and routes with views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir, providing a mix of natural beauty and varied surfaces.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a quick jog in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, Fuente Obejuna offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a shorter, less strenuous run. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001022505/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-gracia-circular-from-fuente-obejuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Senora de Gracia - circular from Fuente Obejuna&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.0 km path that provides a quick and enjoyable outing from the town.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging running routes available in the area?
A: For runners seeking a greater challenge, Fuente Obejuna has several longer and more difficult options. While the most popular difficult route is mentioned in the guide's introduction, you can find other moderate to difficult trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31456313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroja-Pueblonuevo loop from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 10.59 km run with significant elevation changes, providing a good workout amidst varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Fuente Obejuna?
A: Absolutely! Circular routes are a great way to explore without retracing your steps. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001022505/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-gracia-circular-from-fuente-obejuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Senora de Gracia - circular from Fuente Obejuna&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0 km circular path. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Granja de Torrehermosa&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate 8.0 km circular trail through local landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Jogging in Fuente Obejuna offers a chance to experience its rich natural and historical landscape. You'll often run through the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;dehesa landscape&lt;/strong&gt; with its ancient oak trees. Routes along the Guadiato River provide scenic views and you might even spot the ruins of an old water mill at Molino del Hoyo. The Vía Verde also allows you to observe the region's industrial heritage, including old mine shafts. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5354209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Grace&lt;/a&gt; or exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Fuente Obejuna suitable for families?
A: Many of the trails around Fuente Obejuna are suitable for families, especially the easier, flatter sections. The Vía Verde, with its compacted earth surface and gentle gradients, is generally a good choice for family outings. Shorter circular routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001022505/ermita-de-nuestra-senora-de-gracia-circular-from-fuente-obejuna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Nuestra Senora de Gracia - circular from Fuente Obejuna&lt;/a&gt;, are also excellent for families looking for a pleasant and manageable run or walk together.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most natural trails in the Fuente Obejuna area, especially those through the dehesa and along the Vía Verde. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas where livestock (like Iberian pigs) might be grazing or near roads. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural beauty of the trails for everyone.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads for running routes?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't always detailed, Fuente Obejuna and nearby towns typically offer public parking options. For routes starting directly from Fuente Obejuna, you can usually find parking within the town. For trails like the Vía Verde, access points in towns along the route often have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot route details for specific starting points.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Fuente Obejuna?
A: The best time for running in Fuente Obejuna is generally during the &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn months&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant, making for comfortable running conditions. Summers can be quite hot, so if you plan to run then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the midday heat. Winters are generally mild, but some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the running routes in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Yes, Fuente Obejuna and the surrounding towns offer various cafes and restaurants where you can refuel after your run. Many routes either start or pass through these towns, making it convenient to grab a coffee, a snack, or a meal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center&lt;/a&gt; itself has several establishments where you can relax and enjoy local cuisine.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Fuente Obejuna region?
A: The Fuente Obejuna region offers a good selection of running routes, with &lt;strong&gt;over 10 distinct trails&lt;/strong&gt; available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Fuente Obejuna?
A: Runners in the komoot community frequently praise the diverse and scenic landscapes of Fuente Obejuna. Many appreciate the tranquility of the &lt;strong&gt;dehesa woodlands&lt;/strong&gt; and the picturesque views along the Guadiato River. The well-maintained sections of the Vía Verde are also a highlight, offering a smooth and enjoyable running surface through unique natural and historical settings.
tips:
This town is perhaps best known for inspiring Lope de Vega's work "Fuenteovejuna" about the events that took place here in the 15th century that ended with the death of the village commander Fernán Gómez de Guzmán at the hands of the residents.

It has also often been associated with the Roman municipality of Mellaria, although the most recent historical investigations make it clear that this Roman town does not share the same past as Fuente Obejuna and that it was actually located on a nearby hill.

Although this is one of the oldest monuments in Fuente Obejuna, its date of construction is not exactly known, although there are indications that place it between the 15th and 16th centuries. It is dedicated to the Virgen de Gracia, to whom the soldiers brought offerings as thanks when they returned from their battles or asking for protection before leaving. It is said that among these offerings there were human hands and feet, an alligator and even two whale ribs in the hermitage.
